title: The Christian and Work
summary: [1Thessalonians 4:11 And that ye study to be quiet, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you.] During the conference in Stockholm last year, I touched on the topic of the Christian and work. The fact is that we all spend most of our working hours, for much of our adult lives, working. Hence, surely, the Bible has something to say about this all-pervasive and encompassing aspect of our lives. Indeed, the Bible has much to say about the subject because the Lord Jesus Christ is also Lord of our jobs. God gave us our first jobs, regulated the number of days we should work and even adopted the trade of a carpenter for perhaps 20 years of his earthly life. The scripture above tells us that the Lord gave commandments about the Christian and work to the apostles. This would mean that the command to work is also an apostolic command, not just Acts 2:38. Let us unpack these and other scriptures that govern the working lives of Christians.
